summary: Make Agent Workbench view snapshots and stream replay safe across the BFF and React consumer.
rationale: Task 0C intentionally changes the Workbench BFF view cursor contract and the React stream/cache consumer together. The backend must expose a consistent snapshotSeq/viewRevision watermark, and the frontend must start SSE and guard cache replacement from that same boundary.
verification: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_workbench_static_frontend.py tests/test_react_workbench_cutover_gate.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
CI=1 pnpm --dir apps/web-react exec vitest run src/lib/stream/agentStream.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamReducer.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamView.test.ts --run
uv run ruff check src/seektalent_ui tests/test_agent_workbench_stream_consistency.py tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py
deletion_targets: post-build latest_seq reads that can skip events inserted during snapshot construction
silent replay gap events returned as successful HTTP replay responses
frontend stream starts from latestStreamSeq instead of the stable snapshotSeq watermark
risks: Mutation responses must not replace a newer cached Workbench view with an older snapshot.
SSE replay gaps must refetch the Workbench view instead of merging partial stream state.
View v2 must remain bounded to the existing Workbench response surface and avoid Task 0D error-normalization scope.
